basic_details.job_title: NBEMS Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 53 Stenographer, Junior Accountant and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: The National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences (NBEMS) has released its Recruitment 2026 notification (Advertisement No. A-12011/3/2025-Estb dated 02.06.2026) for 53 vacancies across five posts: Deputy Director (Medical), Junior Programmer, Junior Accountant, Stenographer, and Junior Assistant. Online applications open on 29 June 2026 and close on 20 July 2026. The written exam is tentatively scheduled for 05 and 06 September 2026.

application_fee.details: The NBEMS application fee depends on the candidate's category. UR and OBC candidates need to pay a processing fee, while SC, ST, PwBD, and women candidates are exempt. Bank charges, if any, are payable separately by the candidate. | Payment can be made online via debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI of any bank. The fee, once paid, is non-refundable and cannot be adjusted against any future selection process.

age_limit.details: Category Relaxation SC/ST 5 Years OBC 3 Years PwBD (UR) 10 Years PwBD (OBC) 13 Years PwBD (SC/ST) 15 Years Ex-Servicemen 3 years after deducting military service from actual age Central Govt Employees (Group A/B) 5 Years Widows/Divorced Women (Not Remarried) Up to 35 years Widows/Divorced Women SC/ST (Not Remarried) Up to 40 years

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Recognized Postgraduate Medical qualification under Medical Council Act 1956
B.Tech/BE/BCA/DOEACC ('B' or 'C' level)/Degree in Computer Science/IT/Electronics with computer specialization
Bachelor's Degree with Maths/Statistics or Commerce Degree; must qualify NBEMS-prescribed exam
Senior Secondary (12th); Computer proficiency in Windows/LAN; must qualify NBEMS-prescribed exam

qualification.details: Eligibility for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 varies by post in terms of both qualification and age limit. Candidates must meet the essential educational qualification as on the closing date of the online application, i.e., 20 July 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for SC/ST, OBC, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates as per Government of India norms.

salary_details.details: Selected candidates for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 will be placed according to the 7th Pay Commission Pay Matrix Level relevant to their post. The Deputy Director (Medical) post carries the highest pay level among the notified vacancies, while Junior Assistant is at the entry-level Pay Level 2. | Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

Candidates wondering about the NBEMS selection process can note that it differs by post category. For Junior Accountant
Stenographer
and Junior Assistant
selection happens in two stages: a Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by a Computer Knowledge/Skill Test. There is no interview for Group B and C posts.
The CBT consists of 200 objective-type questions worth 200 marks

to be completed in 180 minutes (3 hours). Each wrong answer carries a negative marking of 0.25 marks. The minimum qualifying marks are 40% for SC/ST/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen candidates and 50% for all others.
Candidates who clear Stage-I are called for the Stage-II skill test

which carries 100 marks over a 75-minute duration. Five candidates per vacancy in each category are shortlisted based on Stage-I merit. The minimum qualifying marks for Stage-II are also 40% for reserved categories and 50% for others
and this stage covers Data Entry
Word Processing
Computer Operation
or Stenography depending on the post.
For Deputy Director (Medical) and Junior Programmer posts
the exam scheme and syllabus will be announced separately on the NBEMS website.
how_to_apply: Candidates can apply for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 only through the official website www.natboard.edu.in starting 29 June 2026. The process involves registration, form filling, document upload, and online fee payment. Follow the numbered steps below to complete your application correctly.
Visit the official website www.natboard.edu.in and click on the "Vacancy" link.
Read the official notification thoroughly to check eligibility, age criteria, and post details.
Fill the Registration Form with basic details; a Login ID and Password will be sent to your registered mobile number.
Log in and complete the Application Form with personal, age, and educational qualification details.
Upload a recent colour photograph (max 80KB) and signature (max 50KB) in JPG/JPEG format.
Pay the application fee online via debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI.
Review all entered details carefully, as no changes are allowed after final submission.
Submit the form and download/print the confirmation page, application form, and e-admit card for future reference.

content_sections.heading: NBEMS Recruitment 2026 Quick Summary
content_sections.text: This section gives a fast overview of the NBEMS Recruitment 2026 drive for candidates who want the key facts at a glance. It covers the organization name, total vacancies, application window, and mode of application. Use this table as a quick reference before reading the detailed sections below.

rows.information: National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences (NBEMS)
Deputy Director (Medical), Junior Programmer, Junior Accountant, Stenographer, Junior Assistant

content_sections.heading: NBEMS Vacancy Details 2026 (Post-wise Breakdown)
content_sections.text: The NBEMS Vacancy 2026 notification covers 53 posts spread across five designations under Group A, B, and C categories. Junior Assistant has the highest number of openings at 39, including a backlog vacancy for the ST category. Candidates can check their preferred post and category-wise reservation in the table below.

content_sections.heading: NBEMS Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Eligibility for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 varies by post in terms of both qualification and age limit. Candidates must meet the essential educational qualification as on the closing date of the online application, i.e., 20 July 2026. Age relaxation is applicable for SC/ST, OBC, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates as per Government of India norms.

rows.essential_qualification: Recognized Postgraduate Medical qualification under Medical Council Act 1956
B.Tech/BE/BCA/DOEACC ('B' or 'C' level)/Degree in Computer Science/IT/Electronics with computer specialization
Bachelor's Degree with Maths/Statistics or Commerce Degree; must qualify NBEMS-prescribed exam
Senior Secondary (12th); Computer proficiency in Windows/LAN; must qualify NBEMS-prescribed exam

rows.relaxation: 3 years after deducting military service from actual age

content_sections.heading: NBEMS Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: The NBEMS application fee depends on the candidate's category. UR and OBC candidates need to pay a processing fee, while SC, ST, PwBD, and women candidates are exempt. Bank charges, if any, are payable separately by the candidate. Payment can be made online via debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI of any bank. The fee, once paid, is non-refundable and cannot be adjusted against any future selection process.

content_sections.heading: NBEMS Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the NBEMS selection process can note that it differs by post category. For Junior Accountant, Stenographer, and Junior Assistant, selection happens in two stages: a Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by a Computer Knowledge/Skill Test. There is no interview for Group B and C posts. The CBT consists of 200 objective-type questions worth 200 marks, to be completed in 180 minutes (3 hours). Each wrong answer carries a negative marking of 0.25 marks. The minimum qualifying marks are 40% for SC/ST/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen candidates and 50% for all others. Candidates who clear Stage-I are called for the Stage-II skill test, which carries 100 marks over a 75-minute duration. Five candidates per vacancy in each category are shortlisted based on Stage-I merit. The minimum qualifying marks for Stage-II are also 40% for reserved categories and 50% for others, and this stage covers Data Entry, Word Processing, Computer Operation, or Stenography depending on the post. For Deputy Director (Medical) and Junior Programmer posts, the exam scheme and syllabus will be announced separately on the NBEMS website.

content_sections.heading: NBEMS Salary and Pay Scale 2026
content_sections.text: Selected candidates for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 will be placed according to the 7th Pay Commission Pay Matrix Level relevant to their post. The Deputy Director (Medical) post carries the highest pay level among the notified vacancies, while Junior Assistant is at the entry-level Pay Level 2. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: NBEMS Service Bond and Document Requirements
content_sections.text: Selected candidates must execute a Penalty Bond agreeing to serve NBEMS for a minimum continuous period of two years, coinciding with the probation period. If a candidate resigns before completing this two-year period, they are liable to pay three months' last-drawn salary as liquidated damages. Candidates also need to keep at least 5 colour passport-size photographs, the confirmation page, application printout, and e-admit card for future reference, along with original documents for verification when called.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 (Apply Online)
content_sections.text: Candidates can apply for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 only through the official website www.natboard.edu.in starting 29 June 2026. The process involves registration, form filling, document upload, and online fee payment. Follow the numbered steps below to complete your application correctly. Visit the official website www.natboard.edu.in and click on the "Vacancy" link. Read the official notification thoroughly to check eligibility, age criteria, and post details. Fill the Registration Form with basic details; a Login ID and Password will be sent to your registered mobile number. Log in and complete the Application Form with personal, age, and educational qualification details. Upload a recent colour photograph (max 80KB) and signature (max 50KB) in JPG/JPEG format. Pay the application fee online via debit card, credit card, net banking, or UPI. Review all entered details carefully, as no changes are allowed after final submission. Submit the form and download/print the confirmation page, application form, and e-admit card for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying for NBEMS Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Many candidates lose their candidature due to avoidable errors during the NBEMS online application process. Reviewing these common mistakes beforehand can help you avoid rejection at later stages. Mismatched name/DOB: Always enter your name, father's name, and date of birth exactly as per your High School certificate — corrections are not allowed after submission. Wrong photo/signature size: Upload photographs under 80KB and signatures under 50KB in JPG/JPEG format only; oversized files often cause upload failures. Applying for multiple posts under one form: Each post category requires a separate application; combining details can lead to confusion during document verification. Missing NOC for government employees: Candidates already employed in Central/State Government or PSUs must carry a No Objection Certificate, or risk cancellation. Waiting until the last date: Server load near the 20 July 2026 deadline can cause payment or submission failures — apply early to avoid last-minute rush. Incorrect category claims: Furnishing wrong caste/category certificates leads to outright cancellation of candidature at any stage, even post-appointment.

rows.details: NAMS Building, Medical Enclave, Ansari Nagar, New Delhi - 110029

faq.question: Q1. What is NBEMS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: NBEMS Recruitment 2026 refers to the official Vacancy Notice (No. A-12011/3/2025-ESTB, dated 02 June 2026) released by the National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences for regular appointment to 53 posts across Group A, B, and C categories, including Deputy Director (Medical), Junior Programmer, Junior Accountant, Stenographer, and Junior Assistant.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in NBEMS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 53 vacancies in NBEMS Recruitment 2026 - 2 for Deputy Director (Medical), 1 for Junior Programmer, 4 for Junior Accountant, 7 for Stenographer, and 39 for Junior Assistant. The vacancy count is liable to change as per the official notification.

faq.question: Q3. When will NBEMS online registration open in 2026?
faq.answer: The online registration for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 will be activated with effect from 29th June 2026 on the official NBEMS website at www.natboard.edu.in.

faq.question: Q4. What is the exam date for NBEMS Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The tentative date of examination for NBEMS Recruitment 2026 is scheduled to be held on 5th and 6th September 2026, as mentioned in the official vacancy notice.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility for NBEMS Deputy Director (Medical) post?
faq.answer: For the Deputy Director (Medical) post, the candidate must be below 35 years of age and must hold a recognized Postgraduate Medical qualification approved under the Indian Medical Council Act-1956. Desirable qualifications include training in medical education technology and knowledge of modern evaluation techniques such as MCQ generation and MEQ analysis.

faq.question: Q6. What is the qualification required for NBEMS Junior Assistant post?
faq.answer: For the Junior Assistant post, candidates must have passed the Senior Secondary Examination (12th of 10+2) from a Board/University recognized by Central/State Govt./UT Administration, must have proficiency in computers and basic software (Windows, LAN architecture), and must qualify the examination as may be prescribed by NBEMS.

faq.question: Q7. What is the pay scale for NBEMS Junior Assistant 2026?
faq.answer: The Junior Assistant post is placed at Pay Matrix Level 2 under the 7th CPC, with a basic pay range of approximately ₹19,900 to ₹63,200. In addition to basic pay, employees receive Dearness Allowance, HRA, Transport Allowance, and other Central Government benefits.

faq.question: Q8. What is the age limit for NBEMS Stenographer post?
faq.answer: The age limit for the NBEMS Stenographer post is 18 to 27 years. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD and other reserved categories is applicable as per Government of India norms.
